pages.raw_text:
A. B. Campbell of Finch &
Campbell, who is also vice presi-
dent of the Washington Water
Power Co., denies the statements
attributed to him in the Spokes-
man. Review this morning. The pa
per stated that he had said that
the w. w. P. controlled the vote of
so per cent of its 1900 employees
and that such vote would be cast
for ONO against Pratt.


Mr.. Campbell waxed indignant in
speaking Of the matter and said
he made no statement as to what
the Washington Water Power Co.
would do. beyond that it was
against Pratt as matter of self
protection.


Pratt had recently assailed the
company, Mr.. Campbell says, for
no other reason than to make
cheap political capital, and In view
of this open declaration of hostility
the w. w. p. was opposed to Pratt
as a matter of self defense. The
w. w. p. had no fight against
Pratt, Mr.. Campbell said, until he
courted opposition as grandstand
play to the galleries.


The statement In the Spokesman
Review that Pratt deserved credit
